description: Error cases, usage messages, and edge cases for CLI reimplementation

cases.notes: Returns exit 0 even when not found. Empty JSON array.
API returns RFC 7807 problem details. Script exits 0 - does not check HTTP status.
Checks existence first, warns and skips. Does NOT error.

cases.output:
Usage: hf.cluster.patch.sh spec|labels [cluster_id]

Arguments:
  spec|labels   Which section to increment the counter field in (required)
  cluster_id    Cluster ID (default: current cluster)

Usage: hf.cluster.adapter.post.status.sh <adapter_name> <available> [generation]

Arguments:
  adapter_name  Name of the adapter (e.g., validator, dns, provisioner)
  available     Status: True, False, or Unknown
  generation    Observed generation (default: 1)

Example: hf.adapter.status.sh validator True 1

cases.behavior: Creates cluster with defaults: name=my-cluster, region=us-east-1, version=4.15.0
cases.notes: This is a design choice - create with no args silently uses defaults rather than showing usage

cases.behavior: Creates 1 nodepool named my-nodepool-1 with type m4
cases.notes: Same pattern as cluster create - defaults rather than usage
Known issue: uses GNU awk's asorti() which is not available in macOS BSD awk. Still produces output.

api_error_format.description: HyperFleet API uses RFC 7807 Problem Details for errors
